body.title: [triage] CodingDomain orientation from build_packet.context_shortlist (one artifact, not two)
body.description:
Now that build_packet.py (build.packet.v1) ships a scored context_shortlist that ABSORBS the orientation signature map, the coding loop should orient from the packet — one orientation artifact, not two. Today CodingDomain._initial_message prepends _orientation_prefix() → build_signature_map(ticket) as a SEPARATE block (unseen_university/devices/inference/domains/coding.py); build_packet independently derives the same shortlist via query_file_symbols. That is the duplication the Fable consult flagged (notes/fable-consult-coding-loop-2026-07-04).

**Proposed shape:** CodingDomain builds the packet once (build_packet(ticket)), renders context_shortlist into the orientation prefix (path + per-symbol signature — the same shape build_signature_map emits), and drops the direct build_signature_map call. The packet becomes the single source; the signature-map function can stay as the shared renderer the packet reuses. Fail-open preserved: empty shortlist → empty prefix, loop continues.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/domains/coding.py (_orientation_prefix → render from build_packet), possibly unseen_university/devices/scraps/orientation_classifier.py (share the renderer), devlab/claudecode/build_packet.py (expose a render helper if needed), tests/inference/test_coding_domain_orientation.py (new).

**Design rules:** OOP-first (device design center), log the interface crossing (orientation map size — already logged in build_signature_map), proof-on-close, no-sqlite, fail-open on DB down.

**Scope boundary:** IN — CodingDomain orients from build_packet.context_shortlist; the two orientation code paths collapse to one; behavior-preserving on the rendered map shape. OUT — the packet's gate/measurement/fingerprint consumption inside the loop (this ticket is orientation-rendering only); auto-blocking on a failed gate; architect/editor per-role tiering (separate Fable step).

**Test plan:** a red→green where CodingDomain._initial_message, given a ticket + a stubbed shortlist, renders the packet-derived signature block (green) where the pre-impl path rendered the build_signature_map block directly (assert the message contains the shortlist-derived signatures and that build_signature_map is no longer the direct source — e.g. via a spy/patch showing build_packet is the orientation source). HIGH-inertia: domains/coding.py is design-center — surface for Akien pre-approval before building.

[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
